content.full_text:
March 11, 1977

Honorable Wiley E. Manuel  
Judge, Superior Court  
County Courthouse  
1225 Fallon Street  
Oakland, California 94612

Dear Judge Manuel:

This letter will confirm the advice given you today by telephone, concerning requirements under the Political Reform Act of 1974 as amended, to file a statement of economic interests on leaving your current office as a judge of the Superior Court and on assuming your new office on the California Supreme Court.

Government Code Section 87205(b) provides, in pertinent part, that:

A person who completes a term of office specified in Section 87200 and on the same day begins a term of the same office or another such office of the same jurisdiction is not deemed to assume office or leave office.

It is our interpretation that the phrase "completes a term of an office," as used in the above cited section, includes leaving an office prior to expiration of the full term to assume higher office. Accordingly, as your office is currently specified in Section 87200, you will complete your current term of office and begin your new term on the Supreme Court on the same day, and both offices are of the same jurisdiction1, Section 87205(b) is applicable and you are not deemed either to assume office or leave office. Under these circumstances you are not required to file either a leaving office statement, pursuant to the provisions of Section 87204, or an assuming office statement, pursuant to the provisions of Section 87202. The next statement you are required to file will be an annual statement due by March 1, 1978. This statement will cover the period from January 1, 1977 through December 31, 1977.

1/ The Commission has determined that the jurisdiction of all judges of courts of record, for purposes of filing statements of economic interests, is the State of California.

Please let me know if we can be of any further assistance to you.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
Edgar A. Kerry
Staff Attorney
Conflicts of Interest Division
(916) 322-6444

EAK:mfn
cc: Jack Kavanaugh
    Clerk, Supreme Court
    4250 State Building
    San Francisco, Calif. 94102
